claimText: Arctic Amplification is the phenomenon where the Arctic region warms significantly faster than the rest of the Earth.
evidenceExcerpt: Arctic Amplification is the phenomenon where the Arctic warms significantly faster than the rest of the Earth.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the correct definition of Arctic Amplification.
distractorHooks: The Arctic warms at the same rate as the global average
The Antarctic warms faster than the Arctic
The tropics warm faster than the polar regions

claimText: Reduced albedo due to the melting of reflective ice acts as a feedback loop that drives Arctic Amplification.
evidenceExcerpt: Reduced albedo from melting reflective ice acts as a feedback loop driving Arctic Amplification.
examUse: Conceptual question on the factors driving climate tipping points and polar warming.
distractorHooks: Increased albedo from melting ice drives warming
Melting ice increases the Earth's overall reflectivity
Albedo has no significant effect on polar temperatures

claimText: Lapse rate feedback in the Arctic causes greenhouse gas warming to be most pronounced near the surface, unlike in the tropics.
evidenceExcerpt: Lapse rate feedback in the Arctic causes greenhouse gas warming to be most pronounced near the surface, unlike in the tropics.
examUse: Comparison question contrasting lapse rate feedback in the Arctic versus the tropics.
distractorHooks: Lapse rate feedback causes warming to be most pronounced in the upper atmosphere in the Arctic
Greenhouse gas warming is equally distributed across all atmospheric layers in the Arctic
Lapse rate feedback cools the Arctic surface

claimText: The Water Vapour Triple Effect contributes to Arctic Amplification by creating more cloud cover and acting as a greenhouse gas.
evidenceExcerpt: The Water Vapour Triple Effect contributes to amplification by creating more cloud cover and acting as a greenhouse gas.
examUse: Statement-based question on the role of water vapour in polar climate dynamics.
distractorHooks: Water vapour reduces cloud cover in the Arctic
Water vapour acts primarily as a cooling agent in the Arctic atmosphere
The Water Vapour Triple Effect decreases greenhouse warming

claimText: Arctic Amplification impacts India by disrupting the Indian Monsoon and contributing to sea level rise.
evidenceExcerpt: Arctic Amplification impacts India by disrupting the Indian Monsoon and contributing to sea level rise.
examUse: Statement-based question linking global climate phenomena to regional impacts in India.
distractorHooks: Arctic Amplification has no impact on tropical weather systems like the Indian Monsoon
Arctic Amplification stabilizes and strengthens the Indian Monsoon
Arctic Amplification lowers sea levels around the Indian subcontinent
